The user wants to click on the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.

It's in the third navigation item slot.

The "Docs" link is roughly located at x=300, y=38. Wait, the previous attempt was (303, 38).
Let me look closer.
The "Docs" text is centered in its column.
The column for "Docs" is between x=271 and x=338.

Let's try a slightly different x coordinate, maybe more central to the "Docs" text.

Let's look at the image again.
The navigation items are:
Home: x ~ 145
Pricing: x ~ 224
Docs: x ~ 304
About: x ~ 370
Contact: x ~ 450
Actually, looking at the screenshot, "Docs" is in a box.
The box for "Docs" starts at x=271 and ends at x=338.
The height of the bar is from y=0 to y=83.
Center of "Docs" box: x = (271 + 338) / 2 = 304.5, y = 83 / 2 = 41.5.
Wait, if (303, 38) was outside, maybe the clickable area is different or I misjudged.
Looking at the provided image, (303, 38) seems like it should be right on the word "Docs".
Maybe it wants a more precise center of the element.
Let's try x=304, y=41. Or maybe the text is shifted.
Let's re-read the image coordinates.

"Docs" is white text.
The bounding box for "Docs" link is probably the whole column.
Let's try x=305, y=42.
